mission.title: Install and Configure Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE)
mission.description: Linode Kubernetes Engine (LKE) is a managed Kubernetes service that simplifies the deployment and management of Kubernetes clusters on Linode's infrastructure. It is ideal for users looking for a hassle-free Kubernetes experience with integrated cloud services. Key trade-offs include limited control over the underlying infrastructure compared to self-managed solutions.

steps.title: Install the Linode CLI
steps.description:
The Linode CLI is required to interact with the Linode API for creating and managing your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

curl -O https://linode.com/cli/linode-cli-1.23.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

tar -xzf linode-cli-1.23.0-linux-amd64.tar.gz

sudo mv linode-cli /usr/local/bin/

linode-cli configure

```

> Download the Linode CLI binary.
> Extract the downloaded tarball.
> Move the CLI binary to your PATH.
> Configure the CLI with your Linode API token.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This command creates a new Kubernetes cluster on Linode with the specified version and node count.

```bash

linode-cli k8s clusters create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

```

> Create a cluster with 3 nodes in the US East region.
commands: linode-cli k8s clusters create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your kubeconfig file to allow kubectl to interact with your new Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --text > ~/.kube/config

```

> Retrieve the kubeconfig and save it to your local kubeconfig file.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Ensure that the nodes in your Kubernetes cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the status of the nodes in your cluster.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Configure autoscaling, monitoring, and RBAC for your cluster to enhance its functionality.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml

```

> Deploy the Linode Cloud Controller Manager.

uninstall.title: Delete the Kubernetes Cluster
uninstall.description:
This command deletes the Kubernetes cluster and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

linode-cli k8s clusters delete my-k8s-cluster --force

```

> Force delete the cluster and all resources.

uninstall.title: Remove the Linode CLI
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Linode CLI from your system.

```bash

sudo rm /usr/local/bin/linode-cli

```

> Delete the Linode CLI binary.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Kubernetes Cluster
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your data and configurations. After upgrading, verify that all components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get nodes -o wide

linode-cli k8s clusters upgrade my-k8s-cluster --k8s-version 1.30

kubectl get nodes

```

> Check the current node status before upgrading.
> Upgrade the cluster to the specified version.
> Verify that all nodes are running the new version.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Unable to connect to the server: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your kubeconfig is not set up correctly or the cluster is not reachable.

**Fix:**
Regenerate your kubeconfig using 'linode-cli k8s kubeconfig-view my-k8s-cluster --text > ~/.kube/config'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in kube-system namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Linode Cloud Controller Manager is not deployed.

**Fix:**
Deploy the Cloud Controller Manager using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/linode/linode-cloud-controller-manager/v0.9.5/deploy/ccm-linode-template.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that your cluster does not have enough resources allocated for the requested pods.

**Fix:**
Scale up your cluster by adding more nodes or upgrading existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the image is not available or the credentials are incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check your image repository credentials and ensure the image exists.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Kubernetes features such as PodSecurity admission and improved resource management.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated legacy API versions for certain resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from 1.29, ensure to review the new PodSecurity policies.

resolution.summary: You should see a list of nodes with 'Ready' status when running 'kubectl get nodes'.
codeSnippets: linode-cli k8s clusters create --label my-k8s-cluster --region us-east --k8s-version 1.30 --node-count 3

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools installed and your Linode API token ready.
